metadata.title: Create Version Catalog with Verified Dependencies

story.iWant: to define all dependency versions in gradle/libs.versions.toml
story.soThat: version management is centralized and consistent across all modules
story.tasks: 1. Create gradle/libs.versions.toml 2. Define [versions] section with all 28+ dependencies 3. Define [libraries] section with version references 4. Define [plugins] section (Kotlin, Spring Boot, etc.) 5. Update convention plugins to use version catalog 6. Update framework modules to use version catalog 7. Run `./gradlew dependencies` - verify resolution 8. Cross-check versions against architecture.md Section 2 9. Commit: "Add centralized version catalog with verified dependencies"

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. gradle/libs.versions.toml created with all 28+ dependencies 2. Core stack versions verified: Kotlin 2.2.21, Spring Boot 3.5.7, Spring Modulith 1.4.4, Axon 4.12.1, PostgreSQL 16.10 3. Testing stack versions: Kotest 6.0.4, Testcontainers 1.21.3, Pitest 1.19.0, ktlint 1.7.1, Detekt 1.23.8 4. All framework modules use version catalog references (no hardcoded versions) 5. ./gradlew dependencies shows correct version resolution 6. Version catalog validated against architecture.md specifications

doc.title: EAF v1.0 Decision Architecture
doc.snippet: All 28+ technology versions verified via WebSearch on 2025-10-30/31. Complete compatibility matrix with verification sources and dates. Includes Core Technology Stack, Testing & Quality Stack, Infrastructure & Deployment Stack, and Developer Experience Stack.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Complete version catalog structure with 28+ managed versions including Kotlin 2.2.21, Spring Boot 3.5.7, Axon 4.12.1, testing stack, infrastructure, and developer tools. All versions verified current as of 2025-10-30.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: The system shall provide Gradle multi-module monorepo structure with Spring Modulith boundary enforcement. All dependency versions must be centralized and verified.

doc.title: Coding Standards
doc.snippet: MANDATORY: All dependency versions MUST be centralized in gradle/libs.versions.toml. Never use explicit versions in build files. Use alias(libs.plugins.x) and libs.x dependency notation.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Story defines 6 acceptance criteria for creating version catalog with all 28+ dependencies verified against architecture.md specifications.

artifact.reason: Existing version catalog with 40+ versions, 50+ libraries, 15+ plugins, 9 bundles. Already implements all AC requirements - validation story.
Helper utility for parsing libs.versions.toml in convention plugins. Provides type-safe access to version catalog with in-memory caching. Used by all convention plugins.
Example convention plugin using version catalog. Shows how to reference versions via Catalog.kt helper (e.g., catalog.version("kotlin"), catalog.library("kotlin-stdlib")).
Example module build file using version catalog via convention plugins. Shows clean dependency declarations with libs.bundles.kotlin pattern.
Root build file demonstrates version catalog usage with catalog.findVersion() pattern for Spring Framework override (CVE fix).

gradle.usage: Build tool, Kotlin DSL, multi-module monorepo

kotest.usage: Primary testing framework (JUnit forbidden), BDD specs, property testing

konsist.usage: Architecture boundary validation, Spring Modulith verification

constraints.constraint: MANDATORY: All dependency versions MUST be centralized in gradle/libs.versions.toml (coding-standards.md)
FORBIDDEN: No hardcoded versions in any build.gradle.kts file (zero-tolerance policy)
REQUIRED: Use alias(libs.plugins.x) for plugins, libs.x for libraries, libs.bundles.x for bundles
REQUIRED: All versions must match architecture.md Section 2 verification log (verified 2025-10-30/31)
REQUIRED: Use version.ref pattern for version references in [libraries] and [plugins] sections
VALIDATION: Convention plugins (build-logic/) must use Catalog.kt helper for version catalog access
VALIDATION: Cross-check all 28+ dependencies present and versions correct
TESTING: ./gradlew dependencies must show correct version resolution without conflicts
EXISTING IMPLEMENTATION: gradle/libs.versions.toml already exists with 40+ versions - this is a validation story

interface.name: Version Catalog TOML Structure
interface.signature: [versions] section defines version variables, [libraries] defines dependencies with version.ref, [plugins] defines plugins, [bundles] groups related libraries

interface.name: Catalog Helper API
interface.signature: Catalog(project: Project) - version(key: String): String, library(key: String): CatalogLibrary, plugin(key: String): String

tests.standards: Constitutional TDD mandatory (test-first, Red-Green-Refactor cycle). Primary framework: Kotest 6.0.4 (JUnit explicitly forbidden). For validation stories: Verify existing implementation meets all acceptance criteria through systematic testing. Integration tests preferred for build system validation (real Gradle execution). Zero violations policy: All quality gates must pass (ktlint, Detekt, Konsist). Coverage target: 85%+ line coverage (Kover).

ideas.idea: Integration test: Parse gradle/libs.versions.toml using TOML parser, count entries in [versions], [libraries], [plugins] sections, verify ≥28 dependencies present
Validation test: Extract versions for Kotlin, Spring Boot, Spring Modulith, Axon, PostgreSQL from catalog, assert exact versions match architecture.md Section 2 (2.2.21, 3.5.7, 1.4.4, 4.12.1, 42.7.8)
Validation test: Extract testing stack versions (Kotest, Testcontainers, Pitest, ktlint, Detekt) from catalog, assert match architecture.md specifications (6.0.4, 1.21.3, 1.19.0, 1.7.1, 1.23.8)
Integration test: Search all build.gradle.kts files (framework/*, products/*, shared/*) for hardcoded version strings using regex, assert zero matches (exclude gradle-wrapper.properties)
Integration test: Execute ./gradlew dependencies --configuration compileClasspath for framework/core module, parse output, verify version resolution matches catalog versions (no conflicts)
Cross-validation test: Load architecture.md Section 2, extract all version entries, compare with libs.versions.toml [versions] section, assert all critical versions present and matching
